title: scutellaria bolanderi subsp. austromontana
intro: Scutellaria bolanderi subsp. austromontana, also known as the Alpine Skullcap, is a subspecies of the Scutellaria bolanderi plant, which is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). Traditionally, Scutellaria bolanderi subsp. austromontana has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of anxiety, insomnia, and other nervous system disorders due to its flavonoid and rosmarinic acid content. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cognitive function, and enhancing overall well-being. Scutellaria bolanderi subsp. austromontana is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule for use in herbal medicine, although its effectiveness has not been extensively scientifically studied.
